hunger.label: Hunger
hunger.blurb: Urgency spikes sharply near zero and collapses fast once satisfied. Physiological deprivation is immediately consuming but a full stomach renders the need nearly invisible.

decay.blurb: Depletes steadily over roughly 12 sim hours. Starting near full at the top of a workday, urgency climbs to meaningful levels by midday — a natural lunch trigger.

thirst.label: Thirst
thirst.blurb: Same shape as hunger: near-zero urgency when satiated, sudden spike as the need becomes critical. Dehydration accelerates fast in the final stretch.

decay.blurb: Faster than hunger and easily remedied. Without a drink break, thirst becomes uncomfortable within a morning. The water cooler and vending machine exist for a reason.

bladder.label: Bladder
bladder.blurb: Slightly steeper than the other physiological needs. Urgency is negligible for most of the range but becomes overwhelming near empty, mirroring the real physiology of bladder pressure.

decay.blurb: The fastest physiological decay. Starting at 90, a character hits the urgency zone in roughly 4 sim hours — a natural bathroom rhythm. Drinking events add a small spike on top of the baseline rate.

energy.label: Energy
energy.blurb: A shallower convex than hunger or thirst. Fatigue accumulates more gradually and the urgency near zero is less abrupt, reflecting that tiredness is a slow drain rather than a sudden crisis.

decay.blurb: Drains over a full waking day (~14h from full). Sleep restores energy at 6–8× the decay rate. High stress accelerates depletion beyond the baseline.

stress.label: Stress
stress.blurb: Urgency is low when stress is low and accelerates as it builds. This models stress as a self-amplifying state: the more wound up a character is, the more urgently they seek relief.

decay.blurb: No passive time decay. Stress accumulates from discrete negative events — conflict, embarrassment, Michael — and dissipates through resolution, positive interactions, and rest. A very slow passive bleed back toward baseline occurs only during extended uninterrupted calm.

health.label: Health
health.blurb: Urgency rises sharply as health deteriorates. At full health the pull is negligible; near zero it becomes the dominant concern, overriding most other needs.

decay.blurb: Health has no time-based decay. It changes only in response to events: illness, injury, or sustained stress above a critical threshold. Sleep provides a small incremental recovery tick.

social.label: Social
social.blurb: Both ends of the spectrum generate urgency. Too little social contact creates loneliness; too much creates overwhelm. The trough near the midpoint is the comfortable social range.

decay.blurb: Drifts over roughly 2 sim days in total isolation. Over a normal workday, social naturally trends toward the comfortable midrange. The U-shape means both extremes generate urgency — too-high social also becomes uncomfortable for introverts.

belonging.label: Belonging
belonging.blurb: A gradual, always-present pull toward inclusion. The square-root shape means gains are felt most at low levels; the curve never reaches zero because the need for belonging is never fully extinguished.

decay.blurb: Moves on the timescale of days, not hours. Belonging reflects the cumulative quality of relationships rather than any single interaction, and refills slowly through sustained, meaningful contact.

esteem.label: Esteem
esteem.blurb: Similar to belonging but with a higher baseline, reflecting that self-worth is slightly more chronically active than social inclusion. Urgency is steady rather than crisis-driven.

decay.blurb: The slowest psychosocial drift outside fulfillment. Self-worth erodes almost imperceptibly over a week unless actively reinforced. Event-based hits — praise, humiliation — matter far more than the passive rate.

stimulation.label: Stimulation
stimulation.blurb: Boredom and overstimulation both generate urgency. The higher amplitude means the sweet spot is harder to sustain: most people need to be genuinely engaged to feel comfortable.

decay.blurb: Boredom registers within a day of low novelty. The U-shape means the comfortable zone collapses quickly in either direction: understimulated characters seek novelty; overstimulated ones need quiet.

productivity.label: Productivity
productivity.blurb: The highest baseline of all needs: there is always some pull to be doing something useful. Urgency declines gradually as tasks are completed but never fully disappears.

decay.blurb: Resets on roughly a daily work cycle. The drive to do something useful accumulates overnight and is discharged through task completion. Urgency is context-gated — off-hours, the pull largely disappears for most characters.

fulfillment.label: Fulfillment
fulfillment.blurb: The slowest-moving of all needs. Urgency is always present but mild, and even significant progress only slightly reduces it. Meaning accumulates on a timescale of weeks or years, not hours.

decay.blurb: The slowest-moving need in the simulation. Fulfillment drains imperceptibly tick to tick; its motion is only visible across weeks. Refilled only by rare, high-impact events — genuine creative success, deep connection, recognition that matters.
